channel.title: briefUpdatesMainNLbusinessAndMarkets
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesMainNLbusinessAndMarkets, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: AI can tank teams’ critical thinking skills. Here’s how to protect yours
item.description: AI is transforming how teams work. But it’s not just the tools that matter. It’s what happens to thinking when those tools do the heavy lifting, and w...

item.title: DOJ probes Netflix’s power over filmmakers in Warner deal review
item.description: It's the clearest sign yet that the Trump administration is going beyond a standard deal review as it investigates the merger.

item.title: Mark Cuban Reveals the Most Important Trait for Success. Here’s How to Strengthen It
item.description: It’s not grit, drive or intelligence that bodes well for business triumphs. Here’s what the entrepreneur says is the character trait most important fo...

item.title: In the $3 trillion private credit market, the ‘shadow default’ rate is increasing as more money chases lower-quality deals
item.description: A decline in interest rates offered by the Fed plus excessive demand for private credit investments has reduced yields for investors.

item.title: Why the greatest risk of AI in higher education is the erosion of learning
item.description: Public debate about artificial intelligence in higher education has largely orbited a familiar worry: cheating . Will students use chatbots to write e...

item.title: MacKenzie Scott Donated $20M to a College, No Strings Attached—How It Was Used Has Triggered an Investigation
item.description: A California community college is investigating how part of the unrestricted donation was used.

item.title: Alysa Liu Just Taught a Transcendent Lesson About Success. Everyone Should Pay Attention
JPMorgan admits for the first time it closed Trump’s accounts after the Jan. 6 attack as lender fights his $5 billion ‘debanking’ lawsuit
item.description: “In February 2021, JPMorgan informed Plaintiffs that certain accounts maintained with JPMorgan’s CB and PB would be closed.”

item.title: How to Give Feedback Successfully
item.description: If you’re a manager or a leader, it’s still one of the hardest things you have to do. Here’s how to do it with emotional intelligence.

item.title: NASA delays moon mission to fix rocket, rules out March launch
item.description: NASA engineers found an interruption in the flow of helium — which is needed for launch — in the rocket.

item.title: I completely missed what ChatGPT was doing to me—until an 11-minute phone call made it painfully obvious
item.description: I’ve been using ChatGPT and other AI tools recently for quite a few things. A few examples: Working on strategy and operations for my latest business ...

item.title: He Went Bankrupt Three Times. Then Milton Hershey Built a $50 Million Brand
item.description: Before Hershey became a household name, Milton Hershey spent a decade losing money, businesses, and hope. His fourth try changed everything.

item.title: A new employee missed work on day 4, no reason given
item.description: Inc.com columnist Alison Green answers questions about workplace and management issues—everything from how to deal with a micromanaging boss to how to...

item.title: To protect their businesses, corporate leaders need to speak out about the events in Minnesota and beyond
item.description: Corporate leaders today are stuck between a rock and a hard place. Nobody can see events playing out in the streets in Minnesota and elsewhere and not...

item.title: Trump’s sudden decision to hike his new tariff rate to 15% is ‘something of an eff you’ to the U.K., which thought it had a better deal for 10%
item.description: "It also means some of America’s biggest trading partners, like the EU and Japan, will find themselves exactly back where they were last week."

item.title: Supreme Court tariff ruling has economists even more stressed over the U.S. debt
Why Nvidia is ‘highly motivated’ to keep investing in OpenAI
Asset managers' stocks fell on private-credit fears. What’s worrying investors.
Could these 6 non-AI chip stocks be the next leg of the AI boom?
Trump raises global tariff to 15% in latest response to Supreme Court ruling
